Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act (equalization)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act (equalization)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act to require the Minister of Finance, at the request of the government of a province following a referendum on equalization in that province, to report on the Minister's intention or refusal to enter into discussions with the governments of the provinces and on the discussions that were held, if applicable.

It also provides for consultations to be held with provincial governments prior to any amendment to that Act that would change the method of calculation of the fiscal equalization payments that may be paid under that Act, and for the publication of information respecting those consultations.

It also removes the per capita limit of $60 applicable to fiscal stabilization payments that may be paid to a province. Lastly, it provides for the publication of information respecting the details of the calculation of the fiscal equalization payments and of all amounts paid under the Act.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Equalization and Transfers Fairness Act.
Section 1 of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act is replaced by the following:
This Act may be cited as the Equalization and Transfers Act.
The Act is amended by adding the following before section 3.91:

Subsection.Text: When the government of a province, following a referendum relating to fiscal equalization payments, requests that the Government of Canada reassess, in consultation with the governments of the provinces, the method of calculation of the fiscal equalization payments, the Minister shall cause to be tabled in each House of Parliament, within 90 days after the day on which the request is made or, if the House is not then sitting, on any of the first 15 days of the next sitting of the House, a report stating

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Ministerâs intention to enter into discussions with the governments of the provinces about the method of calculation of the fiscal equalization payments; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Ministerâs refusal to enter into discussions with the governments of the provinces about the method of calculation of the fiscal equalization payments and the reasons for that refusal.

Subsection.Text: If the Minister enters into discussions with the governments of the provinces, the Minister shall cause to be tabled in each House of Parliament, within 120 days after the day on which the request referred to in subsection (1) is made or, if the House is not then sitting, on any of the first 15 days of the next sitting of the House, a report setting out

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the steps that the Minister took to discuss with the governments of the provinces the method of calculation of the fiscal equalization payments;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the details of the discussions, including a list of the persons consulted by the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the steps that the Minister plans to take to address the issues raised during the discussions.

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall not cause to be introduced in Parliament a bill that would change the method of calculation of the fiscal equalization payments that may be paid under this Part unless the Minister has consulted with all provincial governments.

The Minister shall publish all relevant information respecting the consultations referred to in subsection (1) on the website of the Department of Finance and shall ensure that the information is kept up to date.

Section.Text: Section 3.91 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall publish the details of the calculations referred to in subsection (1) on the website of the Department of Finance as soon as feasible after the completion of each calculation.
The portion of subsection 6(1) of the Act before the formula is replaced by the following:
The fiscal stabilization payment that may be paid to a province for a fiscal year is the amount determined by the formula
Subsections 6(8) to (10) of the Act are repealed.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 41:
The Minister shall publish the details of all amounts authorized to be paid under this Act on the website of the Department of Finance as soon as feasible after the payment of such amounts.

Section.MarginalNote: Replacement of âFederal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Actâ
Section.Text: Every reference to the âFederal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Actâ is replaced by a reference to the âEqualization and Transfers Actâ in the following provisions:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the definition cash contribution in section 2, and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the definition sales tax harmonization agreement in subsection 123(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: subparagraph (b)(i) of the definition specified Crown agent in subsection 123(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the definition aboriginal government in subsection 295(1),

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph (a) of the definition refund of tax in subsection 2(1) of the Tax Rebate Discounting Act;

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the portion of subsection 152(10) before paragraph (a),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the definition aboriginal government in subsection 241(10), and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: in the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph (c) of the definition government entity in subsection 107(1), and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: in the Softwood Lumber Products Export Charge Act, 2006,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the description of D in the formula in subsection 99(1.4);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: in the Nova Scotia and Newfoundland and Labrador Additional Fiscal Equalization Offset Payments Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the definition fiscal equalization payment in section 4,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the definition fiscal equalization payment in section 18, and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 14(2) of the Canada Border Services Agency Act;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: in the First Nations Goods and Services Tax Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the definition aboriginal government in subsection 211(1), and

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 26(1) of the Budget Implementation Act, 2000;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: in the Canada Revenue Agency Act,

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 212(4)(a) of the Canada-Nova Scotia Offshore Petroleum Resources Accord Implementation Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 207(4)(a) of the CanadaâNewfoundland and Labrador Atlantic Accord Implementation Act.
